layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: TWO-ROW VERTICAL LAYOUT with a full-width table spanning the upper 70% of the slide body, followed by a bottom-row split into two zones: a left text callout (60% width) and a right chart placeholder (40% width). The slide uses generous padding on all sides (5% margin from slide edges). The title sits at the top with a visible gap below it. The table is structured as a Marvin table (Option C: Flat Geometry) with three columns: Initiative, Description, Target, and Value. The table uses accent tabs beneath column headers, dashed grey row separators between the two initiative rows, and light grey fills for the Initiative column cells. The bottom-row elements are aligned horizontally at the same top edge, with a small gap between them. The chart placeholder is a simple transparent container (no visible border or fill) reserved for the chart element. The text callout is a white rounded-rectangle container with a thin brand-color border and light grey shadow glow, containing the question text in body font. All elements snap to a 4-column × 6-row grid. The layout preserves the rough relative positioning from the source slide: table dominates the upper area, question callout sits bottom-left, chart sits bottom-right.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 3
  Allocation: [0.7, 0.12, 0.18]

Containers:
  C0 (FULL-WIDTH TABLE (UPPER BODY ZONE)): This container holds the primary content table and occupies the upper 70% of the slide body area. The table is structured as a Marvin table using Flat Geometry (Section 4, Option C) because the content is formal/regulatory in nature (CAPEX/OPEX financial data, operational metrics). The table has four columns: Initiative (narrow, 15% of table width), Description (wide, 35% of table width), Target (medium, 25% of table width), and Value (medium, 25% of table width). The Initiative column acts as the row label column. Column headers are plain bold text in brand primary color, left-aligned, with a small accent tab (brand primary fill, very thin height, narrow width, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square) placed directly beneath each header label, left-aligned. Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line (brand primary color) spanning that column's width, with small white gutters between columns. The table has two data rows: Digitise network (row 1) and ADMS 3.0 (row 2). Row 1 Initiative cell: light grey filled rectangle (no border, no rounded corners, full column width, most of the row height) with bold text 'Digitise network' left-aligned with a small left inset, vertically centered. Row 1 Description cell: bullet list with three items (Sensors on all substations; Platform to coordinate/orchestrate; Automate inspections), body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset, vertically centered. Row 1 Target cell: bullet list with three items (Digital network visibility; Reduction in reactive maintenance OPEX; Maximise asset life), body font, brand text color, left-aligned, vertically centered. Row 1 Value cell: multi-line text (~22k distribution substations × $10m = $220m CAPEX +$2m annual OPEX), body font, brand text color, left-aligned, vertically centered, with the final line '30% reduction in maintenance OPEX ($m)' in bold brand accent color. Between row 1 and row 2: a thin dashed grey horizontal separator line spanning the full table width. Row 2 Initiative cell: light grey filled rectangle with bold text 'ADMS 3.0', same styling as row 1. Row 2 Description cell: bullet list with four items (Upgrade Advanced Distribution Management System; Automate switching, fault location and restoration; Integrate DER/EV/solar coordination; Replace manual operator decisions with automated response), body font, brand text color, left-aligned, vertically centered. Row 2 Target cell: single text block 'Network-wide operational coverage across Vector's distribution network', body font, brand text color, left-aligned, vertically centered. Row 2 Value cell: two text blocks stacked vertically: top block '~$15-25m CAPEX upgrade + $1-2m annual licensing/support OPEX' (body font, brand text color, left-aligned), bottom block is a bullet list with three items (Reduced operator headcount/manual intervention; Faster fault restoration = fewer interruption penalties; 15-20% reduction in system operations OPEX), body font, brand text color, left-aligned. No separator line after row 2. The table fills the container width with small padding on all sides. Row heights are distributed proportionally: row 1 gets 50% of the data area height, row 2 gets 50%. Font size target range: medium, reduced slightly if necessary to fit the densest cells (Description column, row 2) without overflow. All row sections across columns share identical top and bottom edges (hard constraint from row-group detection). The table container has no visible border or background fill — it sits directly on the slide background.
  C1 (BOTTOM-LEFT TEXT CALLOUT): This container holds the question callout text and occupies the bottom-left zone of the slide, positioned directly below the main table. It takes 60% of the available bottom-row width. The container is a white rounded-rectangle with a thin brand primary color border, visibly rounded corners, and a subtle even light grey shadow glow (card lift effect, no hard drop shadow). Generous internal padding on all sides (small inset from container edges). The container height is 12% of the slide height, aligned to the same top edge as the chart container to its right. Inside the container: two text blocks stacked vertically. Top text block: 'Siemens cloud based asset management platform;' in body font, brand text color, left-aligned, regular weight. Bottom text block: 'Under the reg regime, what would these OPEX and CAPEX savings look like over time as value for the business?' in body font, brand accent color (to emphasize the question), left-aligned, bold weight. Text is vertically centered within the container. Font size: medium body font size from presentation_style.txt. The container sits at the slide's left margin (5% from slide edge), with a small gap (2% of slide width) separating it from the chart container to its right. The top edge of this container aligns exactly with the top edge of the chart container (baseline alignment rule B5). The container's bottom edge sits at the slide's bottom safe-area margin (5% from slide edge).
  C2 (BOTTOM-RIGHT CHART PLACEHOLDER): This container is a placeholder for the chart element and occupies the bottom-right zone of the slide, positioned directly below the main table and to the right of the text callout. It takes 40% of the available bottom-row width. The container is a transparent flex column (no fill, no border, no visible shape) — it exists purely as a layout anchor for the chart element that will be placed inside it during execution. The container height is 20% of the slide height (taller than the text callout to its left, reflecting the chart's need for more vertical space). The container's top edge aligns with the top edge of the text callout container to its left (baseline alignment rule B5). The container sits at the slide's right margin (5% from slide edge), with a small gap (2% of slide width) separating it from the text callout container to its left. Inside this container, the chart element (a simple line or area chart showing a trend with a peak in the middle and a plateau at the right) will be placed by the chart executor. The chart should fill the container with small internal padding. No manual legend is needed — the chart is self-explanatory in context. The chart uses brand primary color for the line/fill. The container's bottom edge sits at the slide's bottom safe-area margin (5% from slide edge). This container is execution-deferred — the chart element will be added after the container layout is finalized.

_reference_violations.description: C1 (BOTTOM-LEFT TEXT CALLOUT): text block occupies approximately 25-30% of container inner height — severely under-filled. The container has a large empty white band below the two-line question text, with the empty space significantly larger than the text content itself.

C1 (BOTTOM-LEFT TEXT CALLOUT) container height is only ~12% of slide height as specified, but the actual text content requires much less space, resulting in wasted vertical space. The container is over-allocated relative to its content volume.

_reference_critique: C1 (BOTTOM-LEFT TEXT CALLOUT): text block occupies approximately 25-30% of container inner height — severely under-filled. More than 70% of the container is empty white space below the two text lines. SVGSketcher must increase body font size from current size (appears to be ~12-14pt) toward 18-20pt until text fills at least 65% of the container inner height. The two text blocks should be vertically centered within the container after font size increase. Maintain the specified styling: top line regular weight in brand text color, bottom line bold weight in brand accent color. Ensure generous internal padding is preserved on all sides while increasing font size to fill the available vertical space.
